summary: Market sentiment is cautiously optimistic, with U.S. equities pushing to fresh records as AI-driven momentum offsets geopolitical anxiety, evidenced by the S&P 500 and Nasdaq hitting new highs on the back of surging AI chip stocks. The dominant macro driver is the fragile U.S.-Iran ceasefire, which remains technically intact according to Pentagon chief Hegseth despite active exchanges of fire and UAE air defenses engaging Iranian missiles and drones, while Washington is simultaneously building a maritime coalition to secure the Strait of Hormuz amid Iran establishing its own vessel transit mechanism there. On the sector and stock front, semiconductors are leading gains with Intel and Micron approaching major technical milestones, Johnson & Johnson advanced on a pipeline product breakthrough, AMC shares reacted to its Arena One concert-streaming partnership, and Spirit Airlines began the formal dismantling process following its historic collapse. The primary risks investors are monitoring center on Hormuz strait disruption, which threatens global energy supply chains and has already prompted German export forecasts to flatline, alongside uncertainty over whether the Iran ceasefire can hold given Trump's escalatory rhetoric demanding Iran "wave the white flag of surrender." In the near term, markets appear inclined to continue their AI-fueled rally as long as the ceasefire holds, but any material escalation in the Middle East or closure of Hormuz could swiftly reverse risk appetite and spike oil prices, making geopolitical headlines the critical variable to watch.
